questionText: Per NEC 680.22(A), all 125-volt receptacles that supply power to a pool pump motor must have what type of protection?
explanation: NEC 680.22(A) requires that all 125-volt, single-phase receptacles rated 30 amperes or less and located within 20 feet of the inside walls of a pool shall be protected by a ground-fault circuit interrupter (GFCI). This includes receptacles supplying pool pump motors.
sparkyTip: GFCI is the golden rule near water. Any 125V receptacle within 20 feet of the pool edge needs it — no exceptions for pool pump receptacles!

questionText: What is the minimum distance a receptacle outlet must be located from the inside wall of a pool per NEC 680.22(A)(5)?
explanation: NEC 680.22(A)(5) requires receptacles to be located not less than 6 feet from the inside walls of the pool. At least one 125-volt receptacle must be located between 6 and 20 feet from the pool.
sparkyTip: Think of it as the "6-20 rule" — receptacles must be at least 6 feet away, but at least one must be within 20 feet so pool equipment can be plugged in.

questionText: Which of the following must be included in the equipotential bonding grid for a permanently installed pool per NEC 680.26(B)?
options: Metal parts of the pool structure, metal fittings, and forming shells
Only the pool pump motor frame
explanation: NEC 680.26(B) requires bonding of all metal parts of the pool structure, including the reinforcing steel, metal fittings within or attached to the pool, forming shells, mounting brackets, and metal parts of the pool cover mechanism. This equipotential bonding grid reduces voltage gradients in the pool area.
sparkyTip: Think "bond everything metal" near the pool. The equipotential bonding grid ties all metal together so there is no voltage difference that could shock a swimmer.

questionText: Per NEC 680.10, what is the minimum burial depth for underground wiring that is not beneath a building, slab, or paving and supplies a pool, spa, or hot tub?
explanation: NEC 680.10 specifies that underground wiring shall not be installed under the pool or within 5 feet horizontally of the pool unless necessary to supply pool equipment. The burial depth must comply with Table 300.5, which generally requires 18 inches minimum for direct-burial cables.
sparkyTip: Pool underground wiring follows Table 300.5 for burial depth. For most residential pool installations with direct-burial cable, that means 18 inches minimum.

questionText: Per NEC 680.7, are extension cords permitted to be used with pool pumps and other pool equipment?
options: Yes, as long as they are outdoor-rated
Yes, if they have GFCI protection
No, extension cords are not permitted for pool equipment
Yes, but only if the cord is 25 feet or shorter
explanation: NEC 680.7 prohibits the use of flexible cords and extension cords for pool pump motors and other fixed pool equipment. All pool equipment must be hard-wired or connected via a suitable cord-and-plug connection as permitted for specific equipment.
sparkyTip: Extension cords + pools = never allowed. This is a safety no-brainer. Pool equipment gets a permanent wiring connection, period.
